title: How diffusion generation evolved from pixel U-Nets to transformers, flow matching, and modern video models
description: A narrative arc from denoising diffusion probabilistic models and convolutional U-Net backbones through CLIP-conditioned image systems, latent diffusion, diffusion transformers, flow matching, and source-backed open video and world-model examples.
contextSentence: Generation systems did not jump straight to modern video models — they moved through pixel denoising, stronger conditioning, compressed latents, transformer denoisers, revised training objectives, and finally multimodal video and world-model stacks readers can follow without reading every paper first.
takeaway: Start with denoising diffusion and U-Net backbones, add cross-modal conditioning and latent compression, then swap in diffusion transformers and flow-style objectives before recent open systems extend the same iterative loop to synchronized video, audio, and world simulation.

narrativeScope.title: Narrative scope
narrativeScope.body: This post keeps the historical sequence in one place. Canonical reference pages linked below carry module, concept, and model detail; this narrative summarizes roles in the evolution arc without replacing those pages.

sourceBackedExamples.title: Source-backed examples
sourceBackedExamples.body: Named modern models appear here only with claims grounded in public release materials and model pages on this site. Parameter counts, release dates, licensing, and deployment details can change — confirm those facts in the linked model pages and primary citations rather than treating this post as a product announcement.
